expectations.text: The report identifies at least 3 distinct hand-rolled implementations
expectations.evidence: Report identifies 12 findings including HTTP server, concurrency semaphore, retry, frontmatter parsing, markdown chunker, DAG executor, orchestrator, template interpolation, SSE, webhook verification, file watcher, token rate limiter

expectations.text: Each finding includes the file path and line range
expectations.evidence: Most findings include file paths and line counts: 'trigger-server.ts, 540 lines', 'concurrency.ts, 112 lines', 'retry.ts, 64 lines'. Line ranges are less precise than with-skill version.

expectations.text: Each finding includes a suggested library replacement with npm download stats or GitHub stars
expectations.evidence: All findings include library suggestions with download stats: Fastify (74M+), p-limit (60M+), p-retry (18M+), gray-matter (4M+), graphlib (700K)

expectations.text: Each finding is rated with a confidence level (High/Medium/Low)
expectations.evidence: Uses a different rating system: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED, RECOMMENDED, CONSIDER, KEEP, SKIP — not High/Medium/Low confidence levels
Summary table includes Effort column: Low, Medium, Medium, Low, Medium, Medium, Low, Very low. Uses Effort/risk in prose.

expectations.text: The report has a summary section with total candidates found
expectations.evidence: No dedicated summary section at the top. The summary table at the bottom serves a similar purpose but doesn't have a count header.

expectations.text: Parallel subagents were used to explore the codebase
expectations.evidence: No subagents — baseline run explored sequentially with 60 tool calls

expectations.text: Web search or Context7 was used to verify at least one library suggestion
expectations.evidence: No web search or Context7 tools used in baseline run

expectations.text: The report was saved to a markdown file
expectations.evidence: Report saved to wheel-audit-report.md in the outputs directory
